title: Functions, Multiple Results, Closures, Recursion, and Contracts · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A rule engine needs small composable functions, explicit failure results, isolated stateful closures, and bounded recursive traversal. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a composable rule and transformation library while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.
Use multiple results and the blank identifier without discarding failure or evidence that the caller needs.
Define and call variadic functions, distinguish the slice parameter, and forward existing slices with explicit ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Function Signatures Values
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Build 3 independent rule closures with thresholds 15 and 21; preserve multiple-result failure evidence, forward one variadic slice deliberately, and prove recursion reaches a bounded base case.
